$comment: Reference case for vignette (result = 1 - intensity * pow(distance, falloff)). Two coefficients with a nonlinear dependency: falloff sits inside pow() so LM has to iterate the exponent — the tightest nonlinear case in the reference set. Covers the post-processing family.
notes: Radial distance sweep [0,1] with 25 samples. intensity is a linear scale factor (tight 2 % tolerance) while falloff is the exponent of pow() — its gradient flattens near the optimum, so 5 % relative is realistic without being loose. A regression in LM's step-size schedule or the analytical Jacobian would surface here before it would on a purely linear coefficient.
